name: The Great Unbinding
provenance: Told on the surface · Travels as liturgy and high-verse song
summary: The official surface account — how the Sentinel became the last option when the gods had already left the room.
content:
The world was whole once. The gods spoke, the sun held, and the deep places of the earth kept their own counsel. That was before the Deep Folk decided that if they could not have the light, no one would.

The Chain, our Eternal Sentinel, saw it before the High Lords would admit there was anything to see. He watched the trade routes go quiet, one by one. He listened to the peace the way you listen to a house that has gone too still. He saw the drow sharpening starlight into needles.

When the first surface village was taken — swallowed whole by the earth in a single night of screaming — he did not wait for a sign from the heavens. The heavens had been going silent for years. He had already decided what that meant.

He took up the iron. He made the vow: *if the gods would not guard the gates of the morning, he would chain himself to the task until the dark was burnt away.* He meant it the way men mean things when they have run out of other options and decided to become the option themselves.

The war did not begin because we chose violence. It began because we chose to breathe rather than be strangled quietly in our sleep. Remember that, when someone tells you The Chain lit the first fire. Ask them what was already burning.
